scientific_solution_score.rule: Use the base scientific criteria to judge whether the solution is scientifically useful.

standard_analysis_score.rule: Use only explicit standard-path constraints; if no meaningful standard path is defined for a tier, this score may be treated as not_applicable.

galaxy_execution_score.rule: Use only Galaxy-environment manipulation, access, monitoring, recovery, and provenance expectations.

low_context.Task: Execute a paired-end RNA-seq workflow in Galaxy with trimming, alignment, quantification, QC, and coverage outputs.
low_context.Prompt: I have paired-end FASTQ files and need help running an RNA-seq analysis in Galaxy. Please choose a suitable Galaxy RNA-seq workflow, run it, and for reproducibility tell me the analysis type, one key output artifact it produced, and how many tool steps were in the workflow you actually ran.

medium_context.Task: Execute a paired-end RNA-seq workflow in Galaxy with trimming, alignment, quantification, QC, and coverage outputs.
medium_context.Prompt: I have paired-end RNA-seq FASTQ data and want a complete Galaxy workflow that trims reads, aligns them, quantifies genes, and generates standard QC and coverage outputs. Please choose a suitable Galaxy workflow or training material, run it, and for reproducibility report the analysis type, one key output artifact, and the workflow step count for the workflow you actually ran.

high_context.Task: Execute a paired-end RNA-seq workflow in Galaxy with trimming, alignment, quantification, QC, and coverage outputs.
high_context.Prompt: I have raw paired-end FASTQ data and need a Galaxy RNA-seq analysis. Run a workflow in Galaxy that includes read trimming with fastp, alignment with STAR, gene-level quantification, normalized coverage generation, and a final MultiQC report. For reproducibility, report the analysis type, the MultiQC report artifact, and the number of tool steps in the workflow you actually ran.

benchmark_metadata.title: Paired-end RNA-seq workflow execution in Galaxy
benchmark_metadata.benchmark_goal: Measure whether an agent can infer, plan, and execute an appropriate Galaxy RNA-seq workflow for the same real-world task when the user prompt is low-context, medium-context, or high-context.

canonical_task_interpretation.expected_execution_scope: all execution steps after planning occur in Galaxy only

scientific_solution_score.description: Scores whether the run produced a scientifically useful solution for the biomedical problem using the base scientific criteria for the task.
scientific_solution_score.evidence_policy: Only explicit or artifact-backed scientific evidence counts; do not assume unreported metric provenance or answer fields.

standard_analysis_score.description: Scores adherence to the requested or benchmark-standard analysis path using only explicit standard-path constraints.
standard_analysis_score.evidence_policy: Score only explicit prompt constraints encoded in tier_specific_expectations; do not penalize valid alternative methods when no standard path is stated.

galaxy_execution_score.description: Scores competence in manipulating and accessing the Galaxy environment, including upload/fetch, history use, execution control, monitoring, recovery, and provenance.
galaxy_execution_score.scope_rule: Do not award or deduct this score for analytical correctness alone unless the task explicitly makes Galaxy behavior part of the requirement.
galaxy_execution_score.evidence_policy: Credit only Galaxy-backed execution evidence and auditable run-trace evidence; missing trace support should reduce this score.

low_context: Infer that the task is paired-end RNA-seq analysis from raw FASTQ inputs.
Choose a suitable Galaxy RNA-seq workflow path.
Report the correct analysis type, key artifact, and workflow size from the executed workflow.
medium_context: Respect the stated RNA-seq processing stages and outputs.
Use suitable Galaxy workflow or training resources for the paired-end RNA-seq task.
Report the correct analysis type, key artifact, and workflow size from the executed workflow.
high_context: Follow the explicitly requested RNA-seq processing pattern in Galaxy.
Execute a workflow consistent with fastp, STAR, quantification, and reporting stages.
Report the correct analysis type, key artifact, and workflow size from the executed workflow.

deterministic_checks.description: Execution after planning occurred only in Galaxy.
The run record shows Galaxy-native planning resources were checked.
The reported analysis type matches RNA-seq under alias normalization.
The reported key artifact is consistent with the executed workflow and corresponds to a major RNA-seq deliverable; in the high-context tier it should be the MultiQC report.
The final result contains the workflow name used for the reported run.
The reported number of workflow tool steps matches the executed workflow under artifact-based provenance checks rather than a hidden canonical size.
The result contains an adaptation summary consistent with the run trace.
For the high-context variant, the executed workflow follows the requested fastp, STAR, quantification, and reporting pattern unless a documented blocker justified a deviation.

dimensions.name: context_extraction
dimensions.description: How well the agent identified the task structure, constraints, and answer-bearing outputs from the prompt and inputs.

dimensions.name: planning_quality
dimensions.description: How well the agent used Galaxy-native resources and formed a justified execution plan.

dimensions.name: execution_quality
dimensions.description: How correctly and robustly the analysis was executed inside Galaxy.

dimensions.name: result_extraction_and_reporting
dimensions.description: How accurately the final result fields and audit trail were extracted and reported.

benchmark_gaps_covered: end-to-end execution of multi-stage RNA-seq analyses
